★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.11 4.05 2.96 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.59 0.71 0.57 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.68 0.90 0.65 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.83 2.45 1.74 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.28 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.86
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.43
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.73
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Case-Mix Index Ratio

facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2023-08-21 5 5 0 36 1 36
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2021-10-14 12 3 9 76 1 76
Total weighted health score
46.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (29)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
28 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-11-06 2024-11-15
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-04-17 2024-05-03
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-04-17 2024-05-03
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-04-17 2024-05-03
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2024-04-17 2024-05-03
0807 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides drinks consistent with resident needs and preferences and sufficient to maintain resident hydration. E 2024-04-17 2024-05-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-04-17 2024-05-03
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2023-08-21 2023-10-06
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2023-08-21 2023-10-06
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2023-08-21 2023-10-06
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2023-08-21 2023-10-06
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2023-08-21 2023-10-06
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-04-12 2023-04-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-04-12 2023-04-21
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. C 2021-10-14 2021-11-11
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2021-10-14 2021-11-18
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2021-10-14 2021-11-18
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. F 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2019-02-14 2019-04-10
